name: Auto Push to GitHub on Save
description: Whenever a source file is saved in Kiro, auto-commit and push to GitHub main. This triggers GitHub Actions CI which deploys to insightpulseanalysis.netlify.app automatically.

then.prompt:
A source file was just saved in the SentimentAI project. Execute these git commands in order using the shell tool:

1. git -C "C:\Users\prade\OneDrive\Desktop\InsightPulse\sentiment-ai-platform" add -A
2. Check if there are staged changes: git -C "C:\Users\prade\OneDrive\Desktop\InsightPulse\sentiment-ai-platform" diff --cached --stat
3. If there ARE staged changes, commit: git -C "C:\Users\prade\OneDrive\Desktop\InsightPulse\sentiment-ai-platform" commit -m "auto: sync from Kiro IDE"
4. Push: git -C "C:\Users\prade\OneDrive\Desktop\InsightPulse\sentiment-ai-platform" push origin main

If no changes are staged, skip the commit and push steps silently. Do not ask for confirmation — just execute.
